perturbation.original_text: For providing services as set forth herein, VNUE will compensate Promoter i) Two Thousand Five Hundred Dollars ($2,500.00) for each One (1) Year contract Promoter secures per venue and Five Thousand Dollars ($5,000,00) for each Two (2) Year contract Promoter secures per venue, with payment due to Promoter within Thirty (30) Days from the date on which each such contract is countersigned; and ii) through the issuance of VNUE common stock as set forth below, and for the purposes of Rule 144 such shares of stock shall be deemed to have fully earned by Promoter upon the date of each issuance of such stock certificates by VStock Transfer: Three Million (3,000,000) shares of VNUE common stock shall be awarded to Promoter for performing Promotion Services as follows: Source: VNUE, INC., 8-K, 9/14/2015 For every Five (5) music venues that sign a contract with VNUE, Six Hundred Thousand (600,000) shares of VNUE common stock shall be awarded to Promoter; and If Ten (10) music venues sign a contract with VNUE before January 16, 2016, Promoter will receive an additional bonus of Three Hundred Thousand (300,000) shares of VNUE common stock.
perturbation.changed_text: For providing services as set forth herein, VNUE will compensate Promoter through the issuance of VNUE common stock as set forth below, and for the purposes of Rule 144 such shares of stock shall be deemed to have fully earned by Promoter upon the date of each issuance of such stock certificates by VStock Transfer: Three Million (3,000,000) shares of VNUE common stock shall be awarded to Promoter for performing Promotion Services as follows: Source: VNUE, INC., 8-K, 9/14/2015 For every Five (5) music venues that sign a contract with VNUE, Six Hundred Thousand (600,000) shares of VNUE common stock shall be awarded to Promoter; and If Ten (10) music venues sign a contract with VNUE before January 16, 2016, Promoter will receive an additional bonus of Three Hundred Thousand (300,000) shares of VNUE common stock.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of the monetary compensation clause, specifically 'Two Thousand Five Hundred Dollars ($2,500.00) for each One (1) Year contract Promoter secures per venue and Five Thousand Dollars ($5,000,00) for each Two (2) Year contract Promoter secures per venue, with payment due to Promoter within Thirty (30) Days from the date on which each such contract is countersigned;', creates an in-text contradiction. Section 3, which states 'VNUE shall reimburse Promoter for expenses incurred by Promoter while performing the duties herein', now directly contradicts the amended Section 2, as Section 2 implies the only form of compensation is stock, eliminating any expectation of cash payments, even for reimbursable expenses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: VNUE shall reimburse Promoter for expenses incurred by Promoter while performing the duties herein. Promoter shall deliver to VNUE an itemized accounting of expenses incurred on a weekly basis, and VNUE shall reimburse Promoter within Thirty (30) Days of receipt of such accounting.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly states that Promoter will be compensated with both monetary payments and stock. The changed text removes the monetary compensation clause, stating that Promoter will be compensated *only* through stock. The contradicted text states 'VNUE shall reimburse Promoter for expenses', implying a cash payment. The changed text, implying only stock compensation, directly contradicts the reimbursement clause, as there's no longer any mention of monetary compensation. This is a strong contradiction because it eliminates a previously agreed-upon form of compensation, rendering the reimbursement clause unenforceable under the changed agreement.

perturbation.original_text: At any time prior to the end of the Term, the Parties may agree in writing to extend the Agreement for successive One (1) Year periods (the "Renewal Terms") under the same conditions set forth herein.
perturbation.changed_text: At any time prior to the end of the Term, the Parties may agree verbally to extend the Agreement for successive One (1) Year periods (the "Renewal Terms") under the same conditions set forth herein.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'in writing' to 'verbally' directly contradicts Section 11, which states: 'It is agreed between the parties that there are no other agreements or understandings between them relating to the subject matter of this agreement. This agreement supersedes all prior agreements, oral or written, between the parties and is intended as a complete and exclusive statement of the agreement between the parties.' Allowing verbal agreements now opens the door for disputes based on understandings outside the written contract, violating the 'complete and exclusive statement' provision. This change creates an in-text contradiction because the amended Section 6 permits a verbal extension, while Section 11 insists the written document is the sole source of agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is agreed between the parties that there are no other agreements or understandings between them relating to the subject matter of this agreement. This agreement supersedes all prior agreements, oral or written, between the parties and is intended as a complete and exclusive statement of the agreement between the parties. If any provision in this Agreement is held by a court of competent jurisdiction to be invalid, void or unenforceable, the remaining provisions will continue in full force without being impaired or invalidated in any way.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'in writing' to 'verbally' in the changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the agreement is a 'complete and exclusive statement' and supersedes all prior oral or written agreements. By allowing verbal extensions, the changed text introduces the possibility of agreements or understandings outside the written document, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Promoter agrees to indemnify VNUE for any and all liability or loss arising in any way out of the actions of Promoter taken in the performance of this Agreement. VNUE agrees to indemnify and hold Promoter harmless for any and all liability or loss arising in any way out of the actions, during the term of this Agreement, of VNUE officers, directors, employees, agents or third parties not under the control of Promoter.
perturbation.changed_text: Promoter agrees to indemnify VNUE for any and all liability or loss arising in any way out of the actions of Promoter taken in the performance of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'VNUE agrees to indemnify and hold Promoter harmless for any and all liability or loss arising in any way out of the actions, during the term of this Agreement, of VNUE officers, directors, employees, agents or third parties not under the control of Promoter,' Section 7 becomes internally contradictory with Section 13 'The party in violation of any of the provision agrees to pay to the injured party all court fees, attorney fees, charges and expenses as are deemed fair by the court'. Now, if the promoter violates a provision, then the promoter would have to pay the court fees, attorney fees, charges and expenses. But if the VNUE violates any of the provision (Since Section 7 now has an indemnification only for the promoter and not VNUE), then under which provision should the VNUE indemnify the promoter, because section 7 only mentions the indemnification from the promoter to VNUE and not vice versa
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective successors, heirs and assigns. In case any provision of this Agreement shall be invalid, illegal or unenforceable, the validity, legality and enforceability of the remaining provisions of the Agreement shall not in any way be affected or impaired thereby. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Nevada without giving effect to choice of law doctrine. The party in violation of any of the provision agrees to pay to the injured party all court fees, attorney fees, charges and expenses as are deemed fair by the court. Each party hereto consents to personal jurisdiction in Nevada and voluntarily submits to its jurisdiction in any action or proceeding with respect to this Agreement. Venue for any action arising hereunder shall lie in the state and federal courts located Nevada.
perturbation.justification: The original text includes a mutual indemnification clause where both Promoter and VNUE agree to indemnify each other under certain conditions. The changed text removes VNUE's obligation to indemnify Promoter, creating an imbalance. This directly contradicts the spirit and potential application of Section 13, which states 'The party in violation of any of the provision agrees to pay to the injured party all court fees, attorney fees, charges and expenses as are deemed fair by the court.' With VNUE no longer having a reciprocal indemnification obligation in Section 7, a situation arises where, if VNUE violates a provision, the promoter may be left without recourse for indemnification specifically outlined in section 7, even though Section 13 indicates that the violating party agrees to pay the injured party. This introduces a contradiction and inconsistency in how liabilities and costs are allocated if one party violates the agreement, impacting the agreement's enforceability. This follows the Omissions - In Text Contradiction definition
